pages.full_text:
sent John & Charles to the playground to test the conservation of angular momentum on the roundabout. Then he went to demonstrate himself & came back feeling very sick.
	 Monday 28th May
	 Breezy Breakfast. Warm smell of honey. Trying to decide what to do. lots of places closed on a Monday.
	 He went to Wicken Fen & walked around it. Saw hides, dragonflies, lovely yellow flowers. Black polythene to keep water in. [?Filtering] from fields comes in where pumped up. John bought my birthday present. Simon lost a lens
	 Burwell Church is magnificent, like [?for] Kings College Chapel, beautiful architecture, thin pillars; old wooden carvings of elephants with castles. After looking at the church we stopped the van by the river near the campsite & blew up the boat. John & Charles rowed off down the river in their swim-suits... & came back fighting.
	 We had a barbeque & marshmallows. It's sunny & windy.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-entry, continuing from previous spread. '[?Filtering]' — word partially legible, could be 'Filtering' or 'Silting' or similar. 'For Kings College Chapel' — first word at line-start may be 'for' or 'far'. No handwritten page number visible on this side. Year cannot be confirmed from this page alone; 'Monday 28th May' fits 1990 and 2001 within the 1989–2003 range; position near end of volume (pdf_page 294 of 350) loosely suggests later era but year left at low confidence.

Page is dominated by a glued-in colour photograph of a wetland/fen scene with yellow iris flowers and reed beds. The handwritten caption below reads 'Widen Fen.' — almost certainly intended as 'Wicken Fen' (the place visited on this day), with the 'ck' difficult to read or casually abbreviated. Handwritten page number '38' visible top-right corner.
